sourcesContent:
// Static recipe data — 13 baked goods & dessert recipes

const recipes = [
  {
    id: 1,
    name: "Vanilla Tea Cake",
    image: "https://plus.unsplash.com/premium_photo-1663839331420-8bd922cae76d?q=80&w=687&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
    tags: ["cake", "baking", "classic"],
    ingredients: [
      "All-purpose flour  -  1½ cups",
      "Butter (softened)  -  ½ cup",
      "Sugar  -  ¾ cup",
      "Eggs  -  2 large",
      "Milk  -  ½ cup",
      "Vanilla extract  -  1½ tsp",
      "Baking powder  -  1½ tsp",
      "Salt  -  ¼ tsp",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a loaf pan or 8-inch round cake pan.",
      "Beat softened butter and sugar together until light and fluffy, about 3–4 minutes.",
      "Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Mix in vanilla extract.",
      "In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, and salt.",
      "Alternately fold the dry ingredients and milk into the butter mixture, starting and ending with flour. Mix until just combined — do not overmix.",
      "Pour batter into prepared pan and smooth the top. Bake for 30–35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.",
      "Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack. Serve as-is or with a dusting of powdered sugar, or top with butter cream",
    ],
  },


    {
    id: 2,
    name: "Chocolate Tea Cake",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1709195902163-7eee13e78970?q=80&w=1046&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
    tags: ["cake", "chocolate", "baking"],
    ingredients: [
      "All-purpose flour  -  1½ cups",
      "Cocoa powder  -  ⅓ cup",
      "Sugar  -  1 cup",
      "Butter (softened)  -  ½ cup",
      "Eggs  -  2 large",
      "Milk  -  ½ cup",
      "Vanilla extract  -  1 tsp",
      "Baking powder  -  1½ tsp",
      "Baking soda  -  ¼ tsp",
      "Salt  -  ¼ tsp",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a loaf pan.",
      "Beat butter and sugar together until light and fluffy, about 3–4 minutes.",
      "Add eggs one at a time, mixing well. Stir in vanilla extract.",
      "Sift together flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a separate bowl.",
      "Alternately add the dry ingredients and milk to the butter mixture, beginning and ending with dry ingredients. Mix until just smooth.",
      "Pour batter into prepared pan. Bake 40–45 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.",
      "Cool in pan 10 minutes before turning out. Slice and serve plain or with a dusting of powdered sugar.",
    ],
  },

   {
    id: 3,
    name: "Buttercream",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1558301211-0d8c8ddee6ec?w=600&q=80",
    tags: ["frosting", "baking", "quick"],
    ingredients: [
      "Butter (softened)  -  1 cup",
      "Powdered sugar  -  3–4 cups (sifted)",
      "Heavy cream or milk  -  3–4 tbsp",
      "Vanilla extract  -  1½ tsp",
      "Salt  -  a pinch",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Make sure butter is fully softened at room temperature — this is key to a smooth, fluffy frosting.",
      "Beat butter with an electric mixer on medium-high speed for 3–4 minutes until pale and creamy.",
      "Add sifted powdered sugar one cup at a time, beating on low after each addition to avoid a sugar cloud.",
      "Add vanilla extract and a pinch of salt. Pour in heavy cream one tablespoon at a time until you reach a spreadable consistency.",
      "Beat on medium-high for another 2 minutes until light and fluffy.",
      "Use immediately to frost cakes or cupcakes, or store in the fridge for up to 5 days. Re-whip before using if refrigerated.",
    ],
  },


  {
    id: 4,
    name: "Choco Fudge Brownies",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1606313564200-e75d5e30476c?w=600&q=80",
    tags: ["dessert", "chocolate", "baking"],
    ingredients: [
      "Dark chocolate  -  200g (chopped)",
      "Butter  -  ½ cup",
      "Sugar  -  1 cup",
      "Eggs  -  3 large",
      "Vanilla extract  -  1 tsp",
      "All-purpose flour  -  ½ cup",
      "Cocoa powder  -  3 tbsp",
      "Salt  -  ¼ tsp",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C). Line an 8x8-inch baking pan with parchment paper.",
      "Melt chopped dark chocolate and butter together in a heatproof bowl over simmering water (double boiler), stirring until smooth. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.",
      "Whisk sugar into the chocolate mixture until combined. Add eggs one at a time, whisking well after each. Stir in vanilla.",
      "Sift in flour, cocoa powder, and salt. Fold gently with a spatula until just combined — a few streaks are fine. Do not overmix.",
      "Pour batter into prepared pan and spread evenly. Bake 22–26 minutes. The center should look slightly underdone and a toothpick should come out with moist crumbs (not wet batter).",
      "Cool completely in the pan before cutting into squares. For fudgier brownies, refrigerate 1 hour before slicing.",
    ],
  },
 
  {
    id: 5,
    name: "Churros",
    image: "https://plus.unsplash.com/premium_photo-1713962951510-3816d5903c82?w=600&auto=format&fit=crop&q=60&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxzZWFyY2h8MTN8fGNodXJyb3N8ZW58MHx8MHx8fDA%3D",
    tags: ["dessert", "fried", "spanish"],
    ingredients: [
      "Water  -  1 cup",
      "All-purpose flour  -  1 cup",
      "Butter  -  2 tbsp",
      "Sugar  -  1 tbsp",
      "Salt  -  ¼ tsp",
      "Egg  -  1 large",
      "Vanilla extract  -  ½ tsp",
      "Oil for frying  -  as needed",
      "Cinnamon sugar (for rolling)  -  ½ cup sugar + 1 tsp cinnamon",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "In a saucepan, combine water, butter, sugar, and salt. Bring to a boil over medium heat.",
      "Remove from heat and immediately stir in flour all at once. Mix vigorously with a wooden spoon until a dough forms and pulls away from the sides of the pan.",
      "Let cool for 5 minutes, then beat in the egg and vanilla until the dough is smooth and pipeable.",
      "Transfer dough to a piping bag fitted with a large star tip (1M or 1B). Heat oil in a deep pan to 375°F (190°C).",
      "Pipe 4–5 inch strips of dough directly into the hot oil, cutting with scissors. Fry in batches for 2–3 minutes per side until deep golden brown.",
      "Drain on paper towels for 1 minute, then roll immediately in cinnamon sugar. Serve hot with chocolate dipping sauce.",
    ],
  },

   {
    id: 6,
    name: "Banana Bread",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1569762404472-026308ba6b64?q=80&w=687&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
    tags: ["bread", "baking", "sweet"],
    ingredients: [
      "Ripe bananas  -  3 large (mashed)",
      "All-purpose flour  -  1½ cups",
      "Butter (melted)  -  ⅓ cup",
      "Sugar  -  ¾ cup",
      "Egg  -  1 large",
      "Vanilla extract  -  1 tsp",
      "Baking soda  -  1 tsp",
      "Salt  -  ¼ tsp",
      "Cinnamon  -  ½ tsp (optional)",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9x5-inch loaf pan.",
      "In a large bowl, mash the ripe bananas thoroughly with a fork until almost no lumps remain.",
      "Stir in melted butter, then mix in sugar, beaten egg, and vanilla extract.",
      "Sprinkle baking soda, salt, and cinnamon over the mixture and stir to combine. Fold in flour until just mixed — do not overwork the batter.",
      "Pour into prepared loaf pan. Optionally, place a few banana slices on top for decoration.",
      "Bake 55–65 minutes until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. If the top browns too fast, tent with foil at the 40-minute mark.",
      "Cool in pan for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack. Slice when fully cool for clean cuts.",
    ],
  },


  {
    id: 7,
    name: "Swiss Roll (Baked)",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1694449052833-57aebb388474?w=600&auto=format&fit=crop&q=60&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxzZWFyY2h8NHx8c3dpc3MlMjByb2xsfGVufDB8fDB8fHww",
    tags: ["cake", "baking", "classic"],
    ingredients: [
      "Eggs  -  4 large",
      "Sugar  -  ¾ cup",
      "All-purpose flour  -  ¾ cup",
      "Vanilla extract  -  1 tsp",
      "Salt  -  a pinch",
      "Baking powder  -  ½ tsp",
      "Whipped cream or jam  -  for filling",
      "Powdered sugar  -  for dusting",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a 10x15-inch jelly roll pan with parchment paper and grease lightly.",
      "Beat eggs and sugar with an electric mixer on high speed for 5–6 minutes until thick, pale, and tripled in volume.",
      "Sift in flour, baking powder, and salt. Fold gently in two batches using a spatula — keep as much air as possible.",
      "Pour batter into prepared pan and spread evenly. Bake 10–12 minutes until the cake springs back when touched and is lightly golden.",
      "While hot, dust a clean kitchen towel with powdered sugar. Flip the cake onto the towel, peel off the parchment, and roll up the cake with the towel from the short end. Let cool completely rolled up.",
      "Carefully unroll the cooled cake. Spread whipped cream or jam evenly, leaving a 1-inch border. Re-roll tightly without the towel. Dust with powdered sugar and slice to serve.",
    ],
  },
  {
    id: 8,
    name: "Swiss Roll (No-Bake)",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1690085645617-3754b13ca06b?q=80&w=687&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
    tags: ["cake", "no-bake", "quick"],
    ingredients: [
      "Marie or digestive biscuits  -  200g",
      "Cocoa powder  -  3 tbsp",
      "Butter (melted)  -  ¼ cup",
      "Condensed milk  -  ½ cup",
      "Whipped cream  -  1 cup",
      "Powdered sugar  -  2 tbsp",
      "Vanilla extract  -  ½ tsp",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Crush biscuits into fine crumbs using a food processor or zip-lock bag and rolling pin.",
      "Mix crumbs with cocoa powder, melted butter, and condensed milk until a dough-like mixture forms. It should hold together when pressed.",
      "Lay a sheet of cling wrap on a flat surface. Spread the biscuit mixture into a thin rectangle (roughly 8x10 inches) on the cling wrap.",
      "Whip the cream with powdered sugar and vanilla until stiff peaks form. Spread evenly over the biscuit layer, leaving a small border.",
      "Using the cling wrap to help, roll up the mixture tightly from the short end into a log shape. Wrap tightly and refrigerate for at least 3–4 hours or overnight.",
      "Unwrap carefully, dust with cocoa powder or powdered sugar, and slice with a sharp knife to serve.",
    ],
  },
 
  {
    id: 9,
    name: "Chocolate Muffins",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1593199970393-10f0f82e2e47?q=80&w=687&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
    tags: ["muffins", "chocolate", "baking"],
    ingredients: [
      "All-purpose flour  -  1½ cups",
      "Cocoa powder  -  ½ cup",
      "Sugar  -  1 cup",
      "Baking powder  -  1½ tsp",
      "Baking soda  -  ½ tsp",
      "Salt  -  ½ tsp",
      "Eggs  -  2 large",
      "Milk  -  1 cup",
      "Vegetable oil  -  ½ cup",
      "Vanilla extract  -  1 tsp",
      "Chocolate chips  -  ½ cup",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a 12-cup muffin tin with paper liners.",
      "In a large bowl, whisk together flour, cocoa powder, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.",
      "In a separate bowl, whisk eggs, milk, oil, and vanilla until well combined.",
      "Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and fold with a spatula until just combined. A lumpy batter is perfectly fine — do not overmix.",
      "Fold in chocolate chips. Divide batter evenly among muffin cups, filling each about ¾ full.",
      "Bake 18–22 minutes until a toothpick comes out with just a few moist crumbs. Do not overbake.",
      "Cool in the tin for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack. Best enjoyed slightly warm.",
    ],
  },

  {
    id: 10,
    name: "Cinnamon Rolls",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1585190775852-3e6bb2b80184?q=80&w=688&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
    tags: ["baking", "sweet", "breakfast"],
    ingredients: [
      "All-purpose flour  -  3½ cups",
      "Instant yeast  -  2¼ tsp (1 packet)",
      "Warm milk  -  1 cup",
      "Butter (melted)  -  ¼ cup",
      "Sugar  -  3 tbsp",
      "Salt  -  1 tsp",
      "Egg  -  1 large",
      "Butter (softened, for filling)  -  3 tbsp",
      "Brown sugar (for filling)  -  ¾ cup",
      "Cinnamon (for filling)  -  2 tsp",
      "Cream cheese  -  4 oz (for glaze)",
      "Powdered sugar (for glaze)  -  1 cup",
      "Milk (for glaze)  -  2–3 tbsp",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "In a large bowl, mix warm milk, sugar, and yeast. Let sit 5 minutes until foamy. Add melted butter, egg, and salt.",
      "Gradually add flour and mix until a soft dough forms. Knead on a floured surface for 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Place in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise 1 hour until doubled.",
      "Punch down dough and roll out into a 12x18-inch rectangle on a lightly floured surface.",
      "Spread softened butter over the dough. Mix brown sugar and cinnamon; sprinkle evenly over the butter.",
      "Roll dough tightly from the long side into a log. Cut into 12 equal rolls using a sharp knife or dental floss.",
      "Place rolls in a greased 9x13-inch baking dish. Cover and let rise 30 minutes. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Bake 20–25 minutes until golden.",
      "Beat cream cheese, powdered sugar, and milk until smooth. Drizzle glaze over warm rolls and serve immediately.",
    ],
  },

  {
    id: 11,
    name: "Kunafa Chocolate",
    image: "https://media.istockphoto.com/id/2186322524/photo/dubai-chocolate-pistachio-paste-and-kunefe-filled-milk-chocolate.webp?a=1&b=1&s=612x612&w=0&k=20&c=a0w9gDhofmoT-f1adwJKeOVEVRWJy4Qm10-Eg4qdr94=",
    tags: ["dessert", "middle-eastern", "chocolate"],
    ingredients: [
      "Kataifi (shredded phyllo dough)  -  400g",
      "Butter (melted)  -  ½ cup",
      "Dark or milk chocolate  -  300g (chopped)",
      "Heavy cream  -  ½ cup",
      "Pistachio paste  -  ½ cup",
      "Sugar  -  1 cup (for syrup)",
      "Water  -  ½ cup (for syrup)",
      "Lemon juice  -  1 tsp (for syrup)",
      "Crushed pistachios  -  for garnish",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Make the sugar syrup first — combine sugar, water, and lemon juice in a saucepan. Boil for 5 minutes until slightly thickened. Set aside to cool completely.",
      "Shred or roughly chop the kataifi dough into shorter strands. Toss thoroughly with melted butter until every strand is well coated.",
      "Heat heavy cream in a small saucepan until just simmering. Pour over chopped chocolate, let sit 2 minutes, then stir until a smooth ganache forms. Mix in pistachio paste.",
      "Press half the buttered kataifi firmly and evenly into a greased baking pan or skillet to form the base layer.",
      "Spread the chocolate pistachio ganache evenly over the kataifi base. Top with the remaining kataifi and press down firmly.",
      "Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 25–30 minutes until the top is deep golden and crispy all over.",
      "Immediately pour the cooled sugar syrup evenly over the hot kunafa. Garnish generously with crushed pistachios and serve warm.",
    ],
  },

  {
    id: 12,
    name: "Chocolate Chip Cookies",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1499636136210-6f4ee915583e?w=600&q=80",
    tags: ["cookies", "chocolate", "baking"],
    ingredients: [
      "All-purpose flour  -  2¼ cups",
      "Butter (softened)  -  1 cup",
      "Granulated sugar  -  ¾ cup",
      "Brown sugar  -  ¾ cup",
      "Eggs  -  2 large",
      "Vanilla extract  -  2 tsp",
      "Baking soda  -  1 tsp",
      "Salt  -  1 tsp",
      "Chocolate chips  -  2 cups",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.",
      "Beat softened butter with both sugars on medium-high speed until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes.",
      "Beat in eggs one at a time, then mix in vanilla extract.",
      "In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt. Gradually add to the butter mixture, mixing on low until just combined.",
      "Fold in chocolate chips with a spatula.",
      "Scoop rounded tablespoons of dough onto prepared baking sheets, spacing 2 inches apart. For thicker cookies, refrigerate dough 30 minutes before baking.",
      "Bake 9–11 minutes until edges are golden but centers still look slightly underdone. Cool on the pan 5 minutes — they firm up as they cool.",
    ],
  },
    {
    id: 13,
    name: "Chocolate Coated Strawberries",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1559715745-e1b33a271c8f?w=600&auto=format&fit=crop&q=60&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxzZWFyY2h8NHx8Y2hvY29sYXRlJTIwY29hdGVkJTIwc3RyYXdiZXJyaWVzfGVufDB8fDB8fHww",
    tags: ["dessert", "no-bake", "quick"],
    ingredients: [
      "Fresh strawberries  -  500g",
      "Dark chocolate  -  200g (chopped)",
      "White chocolate  -  100g (optional, for drizzle)",
      "Coconut oil  -  1 tsp",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Wash strawberries and dry them completely with paper towels. Any moisture will cause the chocolate to seize — this step is crucial.",
      "Melt dark chocolate and coconut oil together in a heatproof bowl over barely simmering water, stirring until completely smooth. Remove from heat.",
      "Line a baking tray with parchment paper. Hold each strawberry by the stem and dip into melted chocolate, letting excess drip off.",
      "Place dipped strawberries on parchment paper. For a decorative finish, melt white chocolate separately and drizzle over the coated strawberries using a spoon or piping bag.",
      "Refrigerate for 15–20 minutes until the chocolate is fully set.",
      "Serve at room temperature for best flavor. Best consumed the same day.",
    ],
  },


  {
    id: 14,
    name: "Strawberry Delight",
    image: "https://plus.unsplash.com/premium_photo-1669293558903-3f76917a2f45?q=80&w=687&auto=format&fit=crop&ixlib=rb-4.1.0&ixid=M3wxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8fA%3D%3D",
    tags: ["dessert", "no-bake", "fruity"],
    ingredients: [
      "Fresh strawberries  -  400g (sliced)",
      "Whipped cream  -  2 cups",
      "Cream cheese (softened)  -  200g",
      "Powdered sugar  -  ½ cup",
      "Vanilla extract  -  1 tsp",
      "Digestive biscuits  -  200g (crushed)",
      "Butter (melted)  -  ¼ cup",
      "Strawberry jam  -  3 tbsp",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Mix crushed biscuits with melted butter until combined. Press firmly into the base of a 9-inch dish or glasses. Refrigerate 15 minutes to set.",
      "Beat cream cheese, powdered sugar, and vanilla until completely smooth. Fold in 1 cup of whipped cream gently until light and airy.",
      "Spread cream cheese mixture evenly over the biscuit base.",
      "Warm strawberry jam slightly and toss sliced strawberries in it to coat. Arrange over the cream layer.",
      "Top with remaining whipped cream, either spread or piped decoratively.",
      "Refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving. Garnish with a few fresh strawberry slices before presenting.",
    ],
  },
  {
    id: 15,
    name: "Mango Delight",
    image: "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1553279768-865429fa0078?w=600&q=80",
    tags: ["dessert", "no-bake", "fruity"],
    ingredients: [
      "Fresh or canned mango  -  400g (diced or puréed)",
      "Whipped cream  -  2 cups",
      "Cream cheese (softened)  -  200g",
      "Powdered sugar  -  ½ cup",
      "Vanilla extract  -  1 tsp",
      "Digestive biscuits  -  200g (crushed)",
      "Butter (melted)  -  ¼ cup",
      "Mango juice or nectar  -  ¼ cup",
      "Gelatin or agar-agar  -  1 tsp (optional, for firmer set)",
    ],
    instructions: [
      "Mix crushed biscuits with melted butter until well combined. Press into the base of a dish or individual glasses. Chill for 15 minutes.",
      "Beat cream cheese, powdered sugar, and vanilla together until smooth. Fold in 1 cup of whipped cream until fluffy.",
      "Fold in half the mango purée or diced mango into the cream mixture. Spread over the biscuit base.",
      "If using gelatin, dissolve in 2 tbsp hot water, let cool slightly, then stir into remaining mango purée. Pour over the cream layer.",
      "Top with remaining whipped cream and arrange fresh mango pieces on top for decoration.",
      "Refrigerate for at least 3 hours until set. Drizzle with mango nectar before serving for extra flavor.",
    ],
  },
];

export default recipes;
